blockingIssues.description: `OpenAIRealtimeWebSocketModel` and `OpenAIRealtimeSIPModel` still treat connect/update as placeholder state flips, so custom websocket URLs, auth/config, session payload application, and SIP attachment semantics are not preserved at runtime despite the new helper coverage.
`session_payload_from_settings()` still drops GA-style nested output audio settings such as `audio.output.voice` and `audio.output.speed`, so realtime session updates lose requested output behavior.
`OpenAISTTModel::start_session()` still lacks the upstream transcription-session handshake/update semantics, so streamed STT parity remains incomplete.

`OpenAISTTModel::transcribe()` still ignores `STTModelSettings`, and `OpenAITTSModel::synthesize()` discards model/voice/speed when producing output, so concrete OpenAI voice adapters do not forward configurable runtime settings.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When a parity feature claims transport/runtime wiring, require at least one assertion on the public or cross-crate runtime path that proves configuration values are actually consumed, not only helper/unit normalization tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The realtime review found new unit coverage for `connection_url`, payload parsing, and SIP `call_id` guards, but no runtime assertion that `connect()`/`update_session()` use those values; helper-only coverage allowed dead transport wiring to appear complete.

rejectedObservations.summary: Did not escalate the voice transcript's missing reads of several upstream test files into a guidance update.
rejectedObservations.justification: The feature preconditions already required reading the pinned Python voice tests before editing the adapters, so this is worker non-compliance rather than missing mission guidance. The concrete runtime gaps are captured directly in the blocking findings.
